title: 10 best herbal decoctions for chills
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for chills are warm, comforting beverages made by steeping herbs in hot water to release their medicinal properties. These decoctions help alleviate chills by stimulating the body's natural warming mechanisms and soothing cold-related discomforts. Examples of herbal decoctions that can help with chills include ginger tea, which warms the stomach and improves circulation, and peppermint tea, which cools and relaxes the body. By sipping on these teas, people suffering from chills can find relief and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with chills because of its antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and relieve discomfort. The decoction's bioactive compounds, including rosmarinic acid and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe the body and calm shivering. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to chills. By addressing these underlying causes, purple coneflower decoctions provide natural relief from chills, helping to promote a sense of warmth and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Purple Coneflower's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to chills.

Immunomodulatory effects: The decoction's active compounds, such as echinacein and cinnamic acid, modulate the immune system by enhancing the production of cytokines, which helps fight off infections that cause chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple Coneflower contains compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the severity of inflammatory responses associated with chills, such as fever and body aches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate body aches, making them effective in treating chills.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relieve symptoms of chills, such as joint pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse 30-60 grams of the herb material with cold running water and drain well before use.
Combine the rinsed herb material with 400ml of boiling water in a large heat-resistant glass container.
Steep for 20-30 minutes, then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool before serving 1/2 cup three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with chills because of its unique properties that help to calm the body's natural response to cold temperatures. The decoction is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses. Additionally, the decoction's warming effects help to increase blood flow and relax muscles, making it easier for the body to regulate its temperature and overcome chills.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum spp. has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms such as fever or chills associated with infections.

Expectorant and antitussive properties: The decoction of Adiantum spp. has been used traditionally to relieve respiratory issues like coughs and bronchitis, which could potentially help reduce symptoms of chills.

Immunomodulatory effects: Some studies suggest that Adiantum spp. may have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defense against infections and reducing the severity of symptoms like fever and chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat colds, fever, and chills due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Heat the measured water in a pot or tea infuser to boiling point within five minutes.
Add the ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for fifteen to twenty minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.
Let the decoction cool to drinking temperature before consuming within thirty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with chills because its menthol content provides a soothing and calming effect on the body. When consumed as a warm tea, it can help to relax the muscles and ease the sensation of coldness, providing relief from chills. Additionally, peppermint's natural warming properties can also help to stimulate blood flow and increase body temperature, further aiding in the alleviation of chill symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Peppermint's antipyretic property helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which can cause body temperature to rise.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of peppermint help to relax muscles and relieve spasms associated with chills, reducing shivering and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and inflammation caused by viral infections that can lead to chills, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to relieve cold and flu symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the peppermint plant is used to make a decoction that can help to reduce fever and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves per 8 ounces of water.
Combine measured peppermint leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with chills because it possesses natural antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and calming properties. When consumed as a warm tea, the flavonoids and volatile oils in lavender work to soothe the body and mind, reducing fever and shaking sensations associated with chills. Additionally, the herb's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be causing the chill, promoting overall well-being and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.
Sedative and calming effects: Lavender's sedative compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, promote relaxation and calmness, helping to soothe symptoms of chills and reduce anxiety.
Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilatory compounds in lavender, such as camphor and borneol, help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and warm the body, reducing feelings of chilliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender are often used in herbal medicine due to their calming and warming properties, making them effective against chills and fever.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are rich in fatty acids and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with chills and colds.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 8 ounces of water into a medium-sized pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil on high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low for 10 minutes of simmering time.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a large mug, discarding solids immediately.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and stir until dissolved, allowing the mixture to steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, let the decoction cool slightly, then drink while warm, taking small sips throughout the day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goat's rue decoctions helps with chills because of its unique ability to warm and invigorate the body. The herb's active compounds, such as coumarin and glycosides, are known to stimulate blood circulation and increase metabolic rate, which helps to raise the body's natural temperature and alleviate feelings of chilliness. Additionally, goat's rue decoctions have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, which can often cause chills in patients. By promoting a healthy inflammatory response, goat's rue decoctions may also help to reduce congestion and alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Goat's Rue decoction has been shown to reduce fever and alleviate body heat in patients with fever-related illnesses, likely due to its ability to increase sweating and promote the breakdown of toxins.

Antimicrobial and antiviral activity: The plant contains compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral effects, which may help combat infectious agents responsible for chills and fever, such as bacteria and viruses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Goat's Rue has been traditionally used to treat inflammatory conditions, including rheumatism, gout, and arthritis. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to relieve symptoms of chills, such as pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with chills.
Barks: The barks contain bioactive compounds that help to combat bacterial infections, which can contribute to the development of chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried gallega officinalis root, 1l of water, and a medium-sized saucepan.
Rinse the dried root with cold water, then chop it into small pieces to release its medicinal properties.
Combine the chopped root with 1l of boiling water in the saucepan, cover with a lid, and simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature, then store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with chills because of its unique composition that combines antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's citral content helps to soothe and calm the body's natural response to infection, reducing fever and shivering. Additionally, lemon verbena's warming properties stimulate blood circulation, increasing heat production and helping to raise the body's internal temperature, providing relief from chills and discomfort. As a result, lemon verbena decoctions can effectively alleviate chill symptoms, promoting overall wellness and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Lemon verbena decoctions have been shown to reduce fever and alleviate chills by stimulating sweating, which helps to eliminate toxins and bring down body temperature.
Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm the body's response to infection or inflammation, reducing feelings of chilliness and discomfort.

Antiviral and antibacterial property: Lemon verbena's antiviral and antibacterial properties can help combat underlying infections that may be causing chills, such as respiratory tract infections, and prevent further spread of the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate chill symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities that can combat chill-causing infections.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers.
Combine the measured verbena officinalis with 200 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten minutes in a heatproof glass container.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the decoction to cool for five minutes before drinking it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to help combat viral and bacterial infections that often cause chills. As the decoction is absorbed into the body, it helps to reduce inflammation and fever, providing relief from symptoms like shivering and sweating. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties can also help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause chills by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.

Decongestant property: The essential oils present in thyme decoctions can help to thin mucus and ease congestion in the chest and nasal passages, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and relieve chills.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been used in traditional medicine to combat fever and reduce body temperature, making them a suitable choice for making decoctions to treat chills.
Barks: The bark of thyme has been used to reduce inflammation and lower body temperature, making it effective in soothing symptoms of chills.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris herb and place it in a small heat-resistant bowl.
Boil one cup of pure water and carefully pour it over the herb, ensuring the bowl is securely covered.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, releasing the bioactive compounds from the herb.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the chill. The decongestant properties in rosemary also help to clear nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and a sense of relief from congestion. Additionally, rosemary's warming effects can help to stimulate circulation and raise body temperature, making it an effective natural remedy for combating chills and promoting overall wellness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to combat invading pathogens that cause chills and fever by inhibiting their growth and reproduction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, including carnosol and camphor, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as body aches and joint pain.

Stimulant properties: Rosemary's stimulant effects can help to warm the body from the inside out, increasing circulation and helping to raise body temperature in cases of hypothermia or chill, making it an effective treatment for chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the rosemary plant are often used in decoctions for chills because they possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fever and body aches.

Barks: Rosemary barks are sometimes used to make medicinal decoctions for chills due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making them useful for treating the discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 tablespoons of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herb's bioactive compounds to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste before serving warm or at room temperature.
Consume 1 cup of the decoction three times daily, as needed, for up to 7 consecutive days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to increase body temperature. The herb contains compounds that stimulate blood flow and dilate blood vessels, allowing for a greater supply of warm blood to reach the extremities and core of the body. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as shivering and sweating. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, lemon balm decoctions can also help to reduce feelings of cold and discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help combat viral infections that can cause chills, reducing their severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, soothe and calm the body's response to infection, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms such as fever and chills.
Antipyretic properties: Lemon balm's antipyretic compounds, including citral and geraniol, help regulate body temperature, reducing fever and chills by promoting sweating and heat dissipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to treat fever and chills due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain essential oils and flavonoids that help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chill, such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning when they are most aromatic and potent.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves with two cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Drink the decoction three times daily as needed, taking one cup each time, and store any remaining liquid in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with chills because they contain compounds that have a warming effect on the body. The saponins and flavonoids in elderflowers, for example, increase blood flow to the skin's surface, helping to raise body temperature and reduce shivering. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry help to alleviate cold-related discomforts such as headaches and fatigue. As a result, drinking an elder decoction can provide quick relief from chills and help to restore warmth and comfort to the body.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Elder decoction's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause chills, reducing their severity.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder decoction reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.

Expectorant properties: Elder decoction's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion, which can be a contributing factor to chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of elder are rich in starch and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating chills by reducing inflammation and promoting sweating.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes contain alkaloids that help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills by stimulating the body's natural cooling processes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Chop 10-15 grams of sambucus nigra into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine chopped sambucus nigra with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 30-40 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain and discard solids, then drink the decoction as needed up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with chills because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The diuretic and expectorant compounds in dandelion root and leaves help to flush out toxins and congestion from the body, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich properties of dandelion soothe the nervous system, reducing symptoms of chilliness such as shivering and cold sweats. By increasing circulation and warming the body from within, dandelion decoctions provide natural relief for chills and associated discomforts.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion decoctions contain compounds like taraxasterol, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever and chills.
Antibacterial properties: The decoction's high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes provides antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections contributing to chills.
Diuretic effects: Dandelion decoctions stimulate urine production, which can help rid the body of excess heat, toxins, and irritants, thereby reducing the severity of chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelion are rich in inulin, a natural prebiotic that can help stimulate digestion and warm the body from within, making it effective against chills.
Buds: Dandelion buds are packed with antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the body's response to feverish chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in amounts of 20-30 grams for every 500 milliliters water.
Rinse the herb material with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris and gently pat it dry with paper towels.
Combine the rinsed herb material with 500 milliliters boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container and let steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into another container to remove any solid particles and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an amber glass bottle refrigerated at 2-8 degrees celsius for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony decoctions helps with chills because of its unique properties that warm and dispel cold. The herbs used in the decoction, such as Shao Yao (peony root) and Chuan Lian Bai Cao (white mulberry leaf), have been traditionally used to treat fever and cold-related symptoms. When taken as a hot tea, the decoction helps to raise the body's internal temperature, thereby dispelling chills and promoting sweating. This natural warming effect also helps to relieve body aches and discomfort associated with chills, providing relief from unpleasant symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peony's anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce swelling and pain associated with chills, making it an effective treatment for conditions like arthritis and rheumatism.
Warming property: The warming effect of Peony helps to dispel coldness and relieve chills by stimulating blood circulation and warming the body from within.

Antiviral property: Peony's antiviral properties help to combat viral infections that can cause chills, such as common colds and flu, by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant have been traditionally used to treat fevers and chills due to their antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the Chinese Peony plant are used to make decoctions that can help to alleviate symptoms of chills and fever, such as sweating and shivering.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried paeonia lactiflora roots and prepare the necessary equipment.
Measure out 15-30 grams of the dried root and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the root with 6-8 cups of water in a pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1.5-2 hours or overnight to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the body's cooling response. The apigenin content in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system. This reduces stress and anxiety, allowing the body's internal thermostat to regulate its temperature more effectively, alleviating chills and feverish sensations. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease muscle cramps and spasms that often accompany chills, providing a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects help to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to chills by reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with chills, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort.

Sedative property: Chamomile's sedative effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate chills, promoting relaxation and helping the body to regulate its natural temperature.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are used because they contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and fever.
Flowers: The flowers of chamomile are used to make soothing and calming teas that help to ease symptoms of chills, such as restlessness and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantities for your desired dosage.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or three teaspoons of fresh chamomile flowers.
Combine the measured ingredients with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their therapeutic properties.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discarding solids and enjoying the warm tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with chills because it has a natural warming effect on the body. The herb contains compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help to alleviate feelings of coldness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties can help to clear congestion and ease coughs, further reducing the sensation of chilliness. Warming and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's warming and anti-inflammatory properties help to increase blood flow, reduce swelling, and alleviate the discomfort caused by chills.

Seeds: Contain a compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relieve cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in quantities of one ounce.
Combine the harvested material with four cups of boiling water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature then refrigerate for at least two hours before use.
Store the chilled decoction in an airtight container and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with chills because it increases blood circulation, which helps to warm up the body from the inside out. The warming properties of ginger also help to counteract the cooling effect of sweat, providing relief from shivering and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 inches of fresh ginger rhizome cut into small pieces.
Combine the ginger pieces in a clean glass jar or container.
Add 8 ounces of boiling water over the ginger and let it steep.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 10-15 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Transfer the decoction to a cup and drink immediately while still warm.

Combine the herb and water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried achillea millefolium herb or three teaspoons of fresh herb with two cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes in a ceramic or glass container to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain and discard solids before drinking the decoction hot, ideally within one hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with chills because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing body temperature. The silymarin compounds present in milk thistle work to alleviate inflammation in the body, thereby helping to calm down fever and shivering. Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress, which can help combat the underlying causes of chills and fever, such as inflammation and tissue damage.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle are used to make decoctions that help to warm the body and relieve cold-induced shivering.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain silymarin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and fever associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried silybum marianum flowers and stems for this medicinal preparation.
Combine the herb with 500ml of boiling water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, allowing the herb to release its active compounds into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chills with herbal decoctions is to combine warming herbs like ginger, cinnamon, and cloves with calming agents like lemon balm and chamomile. Boil 1-2 teaspoons of the herb mixture in 1 cup of water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as a warm tea. This blend helps to increase circulation, reduce fever, and soothe the body.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal decoctions are fever, rheumatism, arthritis, and flu. Decoctions made from herbs like ginger, cinnamon, peppermint, and eucalyptus help alleviate symptoms such as body aches, joint pain, and congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting sweating. These herbal remedies can also help to restore the body's natural temperature and ease discomfort associated with these conditions.
